title: Contagion
overview: When Beth Emhoff returns from a business trip to Hong Kong, she assumes the malaise she feels is simple jet lag. Within days, she is dead, and doctors are confused. A small cluster of similar cases catches the eye of the CDC.

title: 93 Days
overview: The true story of a Nigerian doctor who uncovered the first Ebola case in Lagos in 2014 and the health workers who risked their lives to contain the outbreak.

title: The Waiting Room
overview: An intimate portrait of a single day inside a busy urban public hospital emergency room in Oakland, California, revealing the struggles of patients, families and staff navigating an overwhelmed American healthcare system.

title: Pandemic: How to Prevent an Outbreak
overview: Doctors and scientists around the world race against time to prevent the next pandemic. An in-depth look at the front lines of the fight against influenza.

title: Fire in the Blood
overview: The story of how Western pharmaceutical companies and governments blocked access to low-cost AIDS drugs for the developing world during the 1990s and early 2000s, causing the deaths of millions of people in Africa.

title: And the Band Played On
overview: Based on Randy Shilts' book about the discovery of AIDS in the early 1980s, depicting how scientists, politicians, and the media failed to respond adequately to the crisis.

title: It Might Get Loud
overview: Three electric guitar icons — Jimmy Page, the Edge, and Jack White — come together to discuss and demonstrate their craft, their origins, and their approaches to the instrument.

title: Don't Look Back
overview: D.A. Pennebaker's iconic cinema verite documentary follows Bob Dylan during his 1965 concert tour of England, capturing the folk icon at the height of his early fame.

title: Searching for the Wrong-Eyed Jesus
overview: A road trip documentary through the American South exploring the roots of Southern gospel, country, folk, and alt-country music, featuring musicians and storytellers from the region.

title: Wattstax
overview: A documentary of the Watts Summer Festival concert held in Los Angeles in 1972, presenting performances by Isaac Hayes, the Staple Singers, and other Stax Records artists, intercut with interviews about life in Watts.

title: Festival Express
overview: A documentary of the 1970 cross-country Canadian music festival by train, featuring performances by Janis Joplin, Grateful Dead, and the Band.

title: Tommy Emmanuel: Heart of the Guitar
overview: An intimate portrait of Australian fingerstyle guitar virtuoso Tommy Emmanuel, tracing his childhood on the road with a traveling band to his decades of performing worldwide and connecting deeply with his audiences.

title: Miles Davis: Birth of the Cool
overview: Stanley Nelson's documentary traces Miles Davis' life and music from his St. Louis childhood to his transformation of jazz across five decades, featuring rare archival footage and interviews.

title: Icons Among Us: Jazz in the Present Tense
overview: A documentary exploring the state of modern jazz, featuring contemporary artists who are pushing the boundaries of the genre while honoring its rich traditions.

title: My Octopus Teacher
overview: A filmmaker forges an unusual friendship with an octopus living in a South African kelp forest, documenting what she taught him about connecting with the natural world.

title: Planet Earth II
overview: David Attenborough returns to narrate a second installment of the BBC's landmark nature documentary series, filmed using the latest camera technology to capture never-before-seen wildlife behaviors.

title: 13th
overview: Ava DuVernay's documentary explores the intersection of race, justice, and mass incarceration in the United States, examining the 13th Amendment and its complex legacy.

title: Citizenfour
overview: Laura Poitras' documentary presents a real-time unfolding of Edward Snowden's disclosure of massive NSA surveillance, filmed during his eight days in a Hong Kong hotel room.

title: The Blue Planet
overview: Sir David Attenborough presents a comprehensive overview of the world's oceans, examining the behavior of its creatures and the habitats in which they are found.

title: The Corporation
overview: Based on the book by Joel Bakan, this documentary looks at the modern corporation and concludes — based on its behavior — that it resembles a psychopathic individual.

title: Bring Me the Head of the Machine Gun Woman
overview: A young DJ gets caught between ruthless gangsters and a deadly hitwoman in this kinetic Chilean action thriller.

title: Last Call at the Oasis
overview: A documentary revealing the American water crisis and the scientists, activists, and citizens struggling to secure the nation's most precious resource.
